data.title: Add a loading spinner while the globe initializes

data.description: Create src/constants.ts to hold arbitrary numeric constants — starting with EXAGGERATION_SCALE (currently 100). Currently 100 is hardcoded in multiple places. This file should be the single source of truth for tunable constants so they can be changed without hunting the codebase. Resolves RP-2.
data.title: Add constants.ts for shared magic numbers

data.description: Blow up features.ts (currently ~300 lines, does terrain/imagery/contours/labels) into a features/ directory — one file per layer. Implement a layer registry pattern where each layer self-registers with a common interface (id, name, init, show, hide, destroy). Slim AppState to { exaggeration: 1|EXAGGERATION_SCALE, layers: Record<LayerId, boolean> }. Renderer becomes a thin orchestrator. UI holds zero rendering knowledge. This is the foundational task that makes all future layer additions trivial — one new file = one new layer. Files: features/terrain.ts, features/imagery.ts, features/contours.ts, features/labels.ts, features/registry.ts (or similar).
data.title: Architecture refactor: layer registry + features/ directory

data.description: Contours are rendered at elev*exag height AND Cesium's verticalExaggeration is also applied — double-exaggeration. Fix: render contours at true elevation and let verticalExaggeration handle scaling uniformly. Contours should always be visible in both modes. Depends on RP-4.
data.title: Fix contour double-exaggeration bug

data.description: Replace current dark glass aesthetic (rgba black) with unified white Google Maps style. Create CSS custom properties (tokens) for bg:#fff, border:#e0e0e0, shadow, radius, font. This is a prerequisite for all other UI tasks. All existing elements (search bar, toggle, panels) get reskinned.
data.title: Google Maps UI design system

data.description: Add OpenPlanetaryMap XYZ tiles as the 'satellite' (real Mars today) imagery option alongside the existing terraformed imagery. OPM provides free, public, multi-LOD XYZ tiles consumable via CesiumJS UrlTemplateImageryProvider. Best candidates: OPM Mars Basemap v0.2 (colorized) or Viking MDIM 2.1. Implement as a proper layer in features/imagery.ts (after RP-4). The layers panel toggle switches between terraformed and real Mars. Depends on RP-4.
data.title: Add OPM real Mars imagery as second imagery layer

data.description: Redesign search bar with two states: collapsed (just the input, minimal footprint) and expanded (full results list or feature info card). Clicking a map label populates the search bar and expands the feature card below it showing name, type, diameter, origin. Clicking away collapses to just the bar. Styled per RP-6 (Google Maps tokens). Depends on RP-4 (labels as a layer), RP-6 (design system).
data.title: Search bar: collapse/expand states + feature info card

data.description: Bottom-left floating button (Google Maps layers icon style) that opens a panel. Panel contains: imagery toggle (terraformed vs real Mars, mutually exclusive), and feature toggles (topography lines, exaggerated topography, map labels — checkboxes). Panel styled per RP-6. Extensible: adding a new layer to the registry should surface it in this panel automatically. Depends on RP-4 (layer registry), RP-6 (design system), RP-7 (imagery layer).
data.title: Layers panel: floating button + layer toggles

data.description: A compass rose that rotates to reflect the camera's current heading. Fixed position (top-right or similar). Clicking it resets north-up orientation. Styled per RP-6 (Google Maps tokens). Should update via postRender listener on camera heading.
data.title: Compass widget

data.description: A scale bar that shows real-world distance for a representative screen span at the current zoom level. Updates on camera move. Styled per RP-6. Should reflect Martian surface km (Mars radius ~3,390 km). Fixed bottom-right (or wherever it doesn't conflict with other controls).
data.title: Distance reference bar (scale bar)

data.description: Determine the best imagery source for beautiful high-quality Mars surface rendering and acquire/verify access. Candidates: (1) Casey Handmer R2 tiles already partially integrated at pub-3c6ee3900f804513bd3b2a3e4df337bd.r2.dev — verify tile URL structure, LOD coverage, and completeness; (2) OPM XYZ tiles (Viking MDIM 2.1, OPM Mars Basemap v0.2) — free, public, no auth; (3) Murray Lab CTX raw GeoTIFFs — ~5.6 TB, would need self-hosting pipeline (likely impractical). IMPORTANT: Do NOT download raw Murray Lab GeoTIFFs without confirming a tile-serving plan first — the full dataset is too large to naively self-host. Goal: identify which source gives best visual quality at full LOD range, confirm access, and document the tile URL pattern and Cesium provider type. Deliverable is a documented decision + working tile URL, not necessarily a download. Must complete before RP-4 (arch refactor) and RP-7 (imagery layer) are finalized.
data.title: Research + acquire high-quality Mars surface imagery tiles

data.description: Research all publicly available Mars rover data suitable for display on the globe. Rovers of interest: Perseverance (Jezero, 2021–present), Curiosity (Gale, 2012–present), Opportunity (Meridiani, 2004–2019), Spirit (Gusev, 2004–2010), Sojourner (Pathfinder, 1997). For each: (1) Traverse path — does a GeoJSON or CSV of the rover's path exist? Sources: NASA PDS, USGS GIS, JPL; (2) Image metadata — do per-image coordinates exist (lat/lon of where each photo was taken)? Sources: NASA Mars Rover Photos API (api.nasa.gov), PDS image archives; (3) Panoramas — are stitched panoramas publicly available with location metadata? Deliverable: a documented table of what data exists, what format it's in, and what URL/download path to use. This drives the data format decisions for RP-4 (layer registry) before architecture is locked in.
data.title: Research rover data sources (traverses, image metadata, panoramas)

data.description: Using findings from RP-13: download rover traverse path data and per-image location metadata for all available rovers. Process into GeoJSON FeatureCollections: (1) traverse.geojson — LineString per rover showing full path; (2) images.geojson — Point features per image/panorama with properties: rover name, sol (Martian day), timestamp, image URL or local path, camera type, caption if available. Add a preprocessing script per rover to scripts/rovers/. Output goes to data/processed/rovers/. Format must be compatible with the layer architecture established in RP-4.
data.title: Download and process rover traverse + image location data

data.description: Implement a rover feature layer (features/rovers.ts) using data from RP-14. Two visual elements: (1) Traverse path — polyline per rover rendered on globe surface showing the rover's full path. Color-coded by rover (Curiosity, Perseverance, etc.); (2) Image pins — map pins/icons at each image location. Clicking a pin opens an info card (using the same card style as the feature info card from RP-8) showing: rover name, sol, camera, thumbnail image, and a link or lightbox for the full panorama. Layer is toggleable via the layers panel (RP-9). Depends on RP-4 (layer registry), RP-14 (rover data), RP-8 (info card UI component).
data.title: Rover layer: traverse paths + image pins on globe

data.description: Two related problems: (1) FeatureData in state.ts is a flat monolithic bag that grows with every new layer — doesn't scale. (2) No documented path for raw→processed data format, so adding a new dataset requires reading existing code to guess what format is expected. Deliverables: (A) Define canonical processed data format per layer type — terrain (.f32 binary → TerrainProvider), imagery (XYZ/TMS tiles → ImageryLayer), vector lines (GeoJSON MultiLineString → GroundPolyline), points (GeoJSON FeatureCollection → Billboard/Label/Primitive). (B) Refactor FeatureData so each layer declares its own data dependency rather than a shared bag — e.g. each layer's init() fetches its own data, or data is keyed by layer ID. (C) Write scripts/README.md documenting the raw→processed playbook per type: what tool to use, what output format, where the output goes in data/processed/. This spike should run alongside RP-4 (arch refactor) and inform it — the layer interface design and data loading strategy are coupled.
data.title: Spike: data pipeline conventions + FeatureData refactor

data.description: RP-1 added a loading spinner as an example task. The final design (see docs/mockups/LOAD SCREEN.png) has no spinner — the globe just loads. Remove the spinner markup and any JS that shows/hides it. Should be done as part of or after RP-4 (arch refactor) since the init flow will change anyway.
data.title: Remove loading spinner
